action_items.action: NEW — VIC DADSON SIGNED OFF UNTIL 16 MAY: Mike informed Colin at 10:18 today that Vic 'has been signed off until 16th May. Didn't notify me or HR, just uploaded the doctor note into Bob.' This is a material change from this morning's assumption that her fortnight's sick leave had ended. Actions needed: (1) Confirm HR (Lauren/Bob) have the note and are processing correctly; (2) assess data engineering coverage gap through 16th May — Nemanja reqs discussion deferred; (3) update Colin on next steps for the BI/data work. Do not chase Vic directly.

GDCU-8619 OPERATOR LIST REDESIGN — STILL BLOCKED (Day 21): No Jira update since 30/04 despite today's product sync. Michael Butlin was in the meeting — outcome unknown. If no resolution was reached in the sync, escalate to Colin Brannigan immediately. Mike is already flagging blocker frustration to Colin (10:19 message: 'blockers at the moment are pretty frustrating. Projects either get descoped to a point that they are rubbish, or not done at all') — use the Friday 121 to force a decision if not resolved before then.

REVIEW GDCU-8834 COOL TONES — PARTIAL RELEASE TOMORROW: Moved from In QA to In Product today at 10:02. Chloe flagged in #gdc-product-design-pod (08:55) that Harden will release what he has done tomorrow, with remaining fixes (comment section styling) next week after Jessica provides UX feedback. Jessica replied 'Taking a look at these' at 09:12. Mike needs to review and sign off on the partial release scope before EOD — confirm what is going live tomorrow vs what is deferred.

REPLY TO JESSICA — BANNERS DECISION STILL OUTSTANDING: Jessica asked on 30/04 at 15:05: 'Let me know about the banners please so I'll check with Ciara after.' Relates to GDCU-8928 IUJ Banners. Mike sent a BBC quiz link on 05/05 (WC Predictor research context) but has not addressed the banners. No new DM messages today. Now Day 7 outstanding — resolve today.

REVIEW GDCU-8877 PHONE NUMBERS ADMIN PANEL — DAY 2: Still In Product. Moved to Mike's queue on 05/05 at 16:21. Novica Vukobratovic's implementation carries the 4-eye-flag indicating stakeholder interest. Review and sign off or provide feedback today — should not sit beyond Day 2.

NEW — REVIEW GDCU-8861 BRAND CAROUSEL COMPONENT: Moved from In QA to In Product today at 11:09. Harden Thomas Wallace's dev implementation of the brand carousel (all geos). Hot-flagged (🔥). New item in Mike's review queue this afternoon.

NEW — REVIEW GDCU-8929 SIDEBAR NAVIGATION: Moved from In QA to In Product today at 10:17. Harden Thomas Wallace's implementation of sidebar navigation to Casino and Betting Strategy articles (GX). New item in Mike's review queue this afternoon.

SCHEDULE WC KO CALL — GROUP BETS DESIGN DUE TOMORROW (THU 08/05): Mike agreed with Chloe on 05/05 to run a kickoff call for World Cup Predictor Game and Group Bets with the dev team. Group Bets design is targeting completion tomorrow. If design is ready, schedule KO call for Friday 09/05 or early next week. Do not let this slip past the weekend without a call booked.

CHECK MIGUEL IS BACK & WC PREDICTOR DESIGN STARTED: Miguel was off yesterday (05/05). World Cup Predictor Game design (GDCU-8935) must start this week with a target of Tue 12/05. No Slack activity from Miguel seen today. Confirm he is back and has the brief — if not, the 12/05 design target is at risk.

KARL CHECK-IN THIS WEEK — WC DATA DEPENDENCIES: Mike told Chloe on 05/05 he has a check-in with Karl 'this week to confirm' the World Cup matrix data (fixture dating & resulting) is exposed — a key dependency for both Group Bets and WC Predictor Game. Still outstanding. Complete today so the KO call with devs has the correct data picture.

PREPARE FOR FRIDAY 121 WITH COLIN — BLOCKERS AGENDA: Mike messaged Colin at 10:19 with 'blockers at the moment are pretty frustrating. Projects either get descoped to a point that they are rubbish, or not done at all.' Prepare a clear brief for Friday: GDCU-8619 (Day 21 — Michael Butlin gate), Vic coverage plan through 16 May, and any WC dependencies needing C-suite pressure. Be specific about what decisions Colin needs to make or unlock.

MONITOR GDCU-8736 LEARNING HUB IMAGERY — MOVED BACK TO IN PROGRESS: Was in Mike's review queue (In Product) this morning. Moved back to In Progress at 13:01 today — Ciara appears to have pulled it back for further work. Keep an eye on when it re-enters In Product. Sign-off still needed to unblock Carlos Blauth on GDCU-8733.

INTAKE FORM — GABRIEL'S SUGGESTIONS STILL OUTSTANDING: Gabriel Cornoiu's 23/04 suggestions (SEO & Information Architecture for new pages/hubs; operator offer attributes) still unconfirmed. Gabriel not active in channels today. Confirm the intake form has been updated this week.

CREATIVE DIRECTION — GDCU-8846 COSMO SPINS SPRING AD CAMPAIGN: Still unstarted. Josh Kerr feedback: needs rethinking — clearer message, less 'gaming' feel. Ciara is carrying GDCU-8619 (Blocked), GDCU-8947 (Cosmo Cash Machine), and WC work. Provide creative direction so this does not age further.
